challenges.title: Inventory Variables

dialogueIntro.name: Bot Buddy
dialogueIntro.text: This terminal controls the drone assembly bay.

dialogueIntro.name: Bot Buddy
dialogueIntro.text: It needs to log the depot's resources. Create a variable called 'fusion_cores' and set it to 50.

challenges.defaultCode:
# Create the 'fusion_cores' variable below
# Example: variable_name = value

hints.wrongValue: Check your value! We need exactly 50 fusion cores.
hints.misspelled: Almost! Make sure the variable is named exactly 'fusion_cores' with an 's'.

challenges.title: Power Loops

dialogueIntro.name: Bot Buddy
dialogueIntro.text: Oh no, the main power loop is broken!

dialogueIntro.name: Bot Buddy
dialogueIntro.text: It needs exactly 10 power units to boot. Can you write a loop that prints exactly 'Power level: 10' at the end?

challenges.defaultCode:
# The terminal needs exactly 10 power units to reboot.
# Fix the loop below to run 10 times.

power = 0
for i in range(5):
    power += 1
    print(f"Power level: {power}")

hints.range5: Your loop structure is perfect, but look closely at the range! It's only running 5 times instead of 10.

challenges.title: Coolant Conditionals

dialogueIntro.name: Bot Buddy
dialogueIntro.text: The reactor coolant terminal is locked.

dialogueIntro.name: Bot Buddy
dialogueIntro.text: Write an if/else statement. If reactor_temp is greater than 100, print 'CRITICAL'. Else, print 'STABLE'.

challenges.defaultCode:
reactor_temp = 150

# Write your if/else statement below:

hints.wrongCondition: Check your condition! We need to know if the temp is GREATER than 100.

challenges.title: Data Core: Pandas Cleaning

dialogueIntro.name: Bot Buddy
dialogueIntro.text: We've reached the Data Core, but the logs are corrupted.

dialogueIntro.name: Bot Buddy
dialogueIntro.text: Import 'pandas' and use 'df.dropna()' to remove the corrupted null entries from the signal log.

challenges.defaultCode:
import pandas as pd
import io

# Corrupted Signal Data
csv_data = """timestamp,signal_strength,noise
10:00,85,0.1
10:01,,0.2
10:02,70,
10:03,90,0.05"""

df = pd.read_csv(io.StringIO(csv_data))

# Clean the data below using df.dropna()
# Then print(df)

hints.missingImport: Don't forget to 'import pandas as pd'!
hints.cleaning: Try using df = df.dropna() to remove rows with empty values.

challenges.title: Data Core: System Statistics

dialogueIntro.name: Bot Buddy
dialogueIntro.text: The cooling fans are vibrating out of sync.

dialogueIntro.name: Bot Buddy
dialogueIntro.text: Calculate the average (mean) RPM from the 'fan_speeds' list and store it in a variable called 'avg_rpm'.

challenges.defaultCode:
fan_speeds = [3200, 3150, 3300, 3100, 3250]

# Calculate the mean (average) of the list below
# avg_rpm = ...

hints.math: To find the average, sum the list and divide by its length: sum(list) / len(list).

challenges.title: Data Core: Orbital Alignment

dialogueIntro.name: Bot Buddy
dialogueIntro.text: Final Boss! The satellite dish is misaligned.

dialogueIntro.name: Bot Buddy
dialogueIntro.text: We need the dot product of the 'current_vector' and 'target_vector' to calculate the alignment delta.

challenges.defaultCode:
current_vector = [1, 0, 2]
target_vector = [3, 4, 1]

# Calculate the dot product (a1*b1 + a2*b2 + a3*b3)
# alignment_delta = ...

hints.dot_product: Multiply corresponding elements and then add them all together: (1*3) + (0*4) + (2*1).
